response.text:
I added the harmonics power measurement and plotted it.

What I see now:
- The scope still shows your existing eye diagram in Plot 1.
- A new power measurement badge appears on the right as Power 1.
- Power 1 is set to Harmonics on CH1 using voltage as the harmonics source.
- The harmonics results shown include:
  - Hcount: 50
  - F1 Mag: 168.8 mV
  - F3 Mag: 180.4 mV
  - THD-F: 208.4%
  - THD-R: 90.15%
  - RMS: 1.371 V
  - Freq: 7.855 MHz

Important detail:
- The harmonics measurement was added successfully.
- The plot source PLOT2 was assigned to POWER1 successfully by SCPI.
- But in this screenshot, the visible plot area is still Plot 1 - Eye Diagram (Meas 4). So the harmonics graph is not the main visible plot yet; it looks like the harmonics result exists as a measurement badge, while the screen is still displaying the eye diagram plot window.

What I did:
- Added Power 1
- Set Power 1 type to Harmonics
- Set harmonics voltage source to CH1
- Set harmonics source type to Voltage
- Added Plot 2
- Set Plot 2 source to Power 1

If you want, I can now try to make the harmonics plot the active visible plot on screen and hide the eye diagram so only the harmonics graph is shown.
